key_insights.insight: Obsession, when channeled correctly, can drive individuals to overcome seemingly insurmountable obstacles.
key_insights.quote: Obsession is a powerful force, isn't it? Because it means that one can bang their head against an immovable object over and over again until the immovable object moves out its way.

key_insights.insight: It's important to be true to yourself, especially when dealing with criticism, as trying to please everyone is an impossible task.
key_insights.quote: Be true to yourself and you will never fall.

key_insights.insight: The struggles and hardships of early career stages build resilience that will be valuable later.
key_insights.quote: It's great to do this because one day you'll get tested in a different way. And you'll be mentally stronger.

key_insights.insight: Success requires putting in the work, showing up consistently, and always striving to improve.
key_insights.quote: This is, I think how you make it exciting is you got to put the work in. Don't get tired. You got to show up and you always got to show up.

key_insights.insight: Positive feedback and support from loved ones, especially family, can be crucial for building confidence and achieving goals.
key_insights.quote: She was part of my life and I think part of what built my confidence.

key_insights.insight: Don't underestimate the power of setting specific, even audacious, goals, as they can drive you to take the necessary steps to achieve them.
key_insights.quote: I wanna be on Saturday Night Live every year, all of my birthdays. Any wish that I could make, that's what I wished, that I could be on Saturday Night Live.

key_insights.insight: Navigating criticism and negativity is part of success and requires toughening up, staying focused, and believing in oneself.
key_insights.quote: And you got to, again, just toughen up and get through it and just keep your head down and keep being funny and just keep doing things and keep being creative.

key_insights.insight: Fame is fun, but real satisfaction comes from creating meaningful work you are proud of.
key_insights.quote: But it's also, at the end of the day, it's the work and the stuff that you do and the stuff that comes out of it where I'm most proud of where I'm like, oh, I get to do this.

key_insights.insight: A life well-lived is about being nice to people, giving when you can, and making people smile.
key_insights.quote: Be nice to people. Give when you can. Make people smile.

key_insights.insight: For true success, pursue what you genuinely want to do. If you do what you love, success will follow.
key_insights.quote: Then go do what you want to do. I think that will make you successful. Do what you want to do. If you do what you want to do, you'll be successful.

actionable_advice.advice: Embrace small jobs and experiences as a foundation for future growth.
actionable_advice.how_to: Take on any opportunity, even if it seems insignificant or pays little, as it builds skills and provides valuable experience.

actionable_advice.advice: When feeling lost, go back and re-evaluate your purpose.
actionable_advice.how_to: Ask yourself what you really want to do and why. Use the answers to guide your actions.

actionable_advice.advice: If you don't get the opportunity you want, try another route or alter your approach.
actionable_advice.how_to: Do not give up if you fail, just keep practicing and working on getting a different opportunity.

memorable_stories: Jimmy's story of following Jerry Seinfeld's performance at The Improv with his Troll Doll act.
Jimmy's description of his mom's dramatic choking incident at a restaurant.
Jimmy's story of how he and Mick Jagger created the mirror sketch on SNL.
contrarian_claims: TV is not dying, and remains as powerful as ever, it just has a different format now.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Jimmy Fallon: I Didn't Expect It To Be This Brutal! Nothing Worked. I Was Starving & Had A Suicide Pact With Myself!
